title: Sitael Accelerates Growth in Space:New Missions, Industrial Capacity and a Trajectory Towards 2031
summary: Sitael has announced a growth plan targeting €200 million in revenues by 2031, backed by over €150 million in backlog and nine planned launches by 2030. The company is expanding its industrial capacity and mission portfolio to achieve this trajectory.

title: Schaeffler AG joins forces with Spire to build sovereign European satellites
summary: Schaeffler AG has partnered with Spire Global to enhance satellite production in Germany, aiming to strengthen sovereign European satellite capabilities. The collaboration focuses on scaling up satellite manufacturing with a major German automotive systems manufacturer.

title: Starcloud’s path to 88,000 computing satellites
summary: Starcloud launched its first satellite, Starcloud-1, aboard a SpaceX mission in November, featuring an NVIDIA H100 compute system. The company aims to deploy a massive constellation of 88,000 computing satellites.

title: Spire expands European manufacturing with Munich plant
summary: Spire Global has opened a new manufacturing facility in Munich to produce up to 100 small satellites annually. This expansion adds to Spire's existing plants in Boulder, Colorado, and Glasgow, Scotland.

title: Europe’s satellite spectrum proposal clouds SpaceX and Viasat plans
summary: Europe proposed reserving two-thirds of 2 GHz mobile satellite spectrum for European operators, which may hinder SpaceX's direct-to-device plans and Viasat's European Aviation Network expansion.

title: Artemis 2 captured the world’s attention. It should change how we think about supply chains.
summary: Artemis 2 has garnered global attention as it marks humanity's return to the moon and highlights the next phase of human spaceflight. The mission is also expected to influence perspectives on supply chain dynamics in the space industry.

title: World’s smallest deployable operational optical ground station proves capability in successful trials
summary: Archangel Lightworks successfully completed field trials of TERRA-M, the world's smallest deployable operational optical ground station. The trials proved its operational capability in laser communications.

title: European space industry watching SpaceX IPO plans
summary: European space companies and investors are closely monitoring SpaceX's plans for an IPO, which could potentially boost investment in other space firms. The move is seen as a significant development for the broader space industry in Europe.

title: Nations opt for a mix of sovereign, bilateral, federated and commercial space capabilities
summary: European nations are adopting a mix of sovereign, bilateral, federated, and commercial space technologies to bolster military readiness. This approach emphasizes sovereignty, independence, safety, and security in space capabilities.

title: SpaceX wins $2.29 billion Space Force contract for military data network
summary: SpaceX has secured a $2.29 billion contract from the U.S. Space Force to develop the Space Data Network backbone. This agreement marks a significant expansion of SpaceX's role in military satellite communications.

title: Every Lego Star Wars set released in 2026 that I wish I had
summary: Lego is releasing multiple Star Wars-themed sets in 2026, featuring interactive models like the AT-AT and Smart Brick-enabled designs.

title: 3 planets will line up in the night sky tonight: Here's where to look for the mini planetary parade
summary: Jupiter, Venus, and Mercury will align in the night sky tonight, creating a visible planetary parade in the western sky at sunset.

title: Laser beams blast through the cosmos | Space photo of the day for May 27, 2026
summary: A space photo from May 27, 2026, showcases laser beams being used for scientific purposes in the cosmos. The lasers are part of a research initiative to study cosmic phenomena.

title: NASA will reveal the Artemis 3 astronauts on June 9
summary: NASA is set to reveal the crew for the Artemis 3 mission on June 9. The astronauts will participate in a docking mission in Earth orbit next year.

title: 'One of the great unresolved embarrassments of physics': It's been 340 years since Newton and scientists still haven't solved the secret of gravity
summary: Scientists have yet to precisely determine the gravitational constant 'Big G' after 340 years since Newton's work. A researcher spent a decade trying to solve this fundamental physics mystery.

title: The Milky Way may have devoured another galaxy named Loki, and astronomers think they've found its remains
summary: Astronomers have identified 20 stars that may have originated from a dwarf galaxy named 'Loki,' which was absorbed by the Milky Way. This discovery suggests the Milky Way devoured another galaxy in its past.

title: Watch 2 Russian cosmonauts take 5-hour spacewalk outside the ISS today
summary: Two Russian cosmonauts, Sergey Kud-Sverchkov and Sergei Mikaev, will conduct a 5-hour spacewalk outside the International Space Station today (May 27). The event can be watched live.

title: Artemis moon base will cover 'hundreds of square miles' with hopping drones and new lunar rovers, NASA says
summary: NASA plans to build an Artemis moon base spanning hundreds of square miles, using hopping drones and new lunar rovers. The agency awarded $1 billion in contracts to initiate the project.

title: 'Masters of the Universe' gets a final trailer, and it's a perfect blend of nostalgia, sci-fi battles, and muscles (video)
summary: The final trailer for 'Masters of the Universe' has been released, showcasing a mix of nostalgia, sci-fi battles, and muscular themes. The trailer features a dramatic line from the protagonist vowing to fight for their family and world.

title: Satellites imaged an underwater volcano erupting — but scientists have no idea what's actually happening on the seafloor
summary: Satellites captured footage of an underwater volcano eruption in a remote ocean region. Scientists lack data to fully understand the seafloor activity.

title: NASA’s Webb Reveals Black Hole That Formed Before Its Galaxy
summary: NASA's Webb telescope discovered a black hole that formed before its host galaxy, challenging the traditional theory that galaxies precede black holes. This finding suggests black holes may have played a role in galaxy formation rather than the other way around.

title: National Institutes of Health Nutrition Education Challenge
summary: NASA's Center of Excellence for Collaborative Innovation (CoECI) is assisting the National Institutes of Health (NIH) in a nutrition education challenge using crowdsourcing. The NASA Tournament Lab will run the challenge for the NIH's Office of Nutrition Research.

title: NASA’s 2026 Lunabotics: Winning Student Teams Engineering Lunar Future
summary: NASA's 2026 Lunabotics Challenge showcased college student teams engineering autonomous lunar robots, aligning with the agency's plans for a sustainable Moon base. The event highlighted resilient, efficient, and autonomous hardware solutions at the Kennedy Space Center.

title: Hubble Spies Faint Irregular Galaxy
summary: The NASA Hubble Space Telescope captured an image of the dwarf irregular galaxy ESO 490-017, located 23 million light-years away in the constellation Canis Major. The galaxy's low surface brightness makes it appear faint against brighter foreground stars.

title: Ever Restless Mount Dukono Erupts
summary: Mount Dukono in Indonesia's Halmahera Island has erupted, ejecting ash, volcanic gases, and volcanic bombs. This is a recurring volcanic activity observed in the region.

title: NASA to Announce Artemis III Crew, Provide Mission Progress Update
summary: NASA will announce the crew for the Artemis III mission and provide a progress update in a live event on June 9. The event will be streamed on NASA+ and YouTube.

title: NASA Astronauts to Answer Questions from Students in New York
summary: NASA astronaut Jessica Meir will answer STEM questions from students in New York during a live Earth-to-space call aboard the International Space Station. The event will be streamed on NASA's YouTube channel on May 28.

title: NASA Provides Update on Moon Base Rovers, Landers, Missions
summary: NASA announced new contracts for lunar rovers and uncrewed cargo landers during a Moon Base event. The agency also shared target launch dates for upcoming missions.

title: Chennai City Lights
summary: A photograph taken from the International Space Station captures Chennai's LED-lit streets at night. The image highlights Earth observations from the space station.

title: New Instrument Used Antarctic Ice Sheet to Probe Extreme Universe
summary: NASA's PUEO mission, part of the Astrophysics Pioneers Program, uses an instrument deployed on the Antarctic ice sheet to detect ultrahigh-energy particles from the universe. This mission aims to probe extreme cosmic phenomena.

title: Following Starship V3 debut, SpaceX prepares for follow up
summary: SpaceX successfully conducted the first flight of its Starship Version 3 this week. The company is now preparing for a follow-up mission.

title: Launch Preview: Starlink and Amazon Leo missions fill manifest
summary: Six orbital launches are scheduled this week, with four Falcon 9 Starlink missions and Amazon's Leo satellite launches dominating the manifest. The launches highlight the growing commercial space activity in low Earth orbit.

title: Vehicle arrivals, science experiments, and maintenance keep Expedition 74 busy through April
summary: Expedition 74 crew aboard the International Space Station has been occupied with vehicle arrivals, science experiments, and maintenance tasks throughout April. The activities highlight ongoing operations and research in low Earth orbit.

title: Shenzhou 23 launches new crew to Tiangong space station
summary: China's Shenzhou 23 mission successfully launched a new crew to the Tiangong space station, which has been continuously occupied since June 5, 2022.

title: Starship Flight 12: Block 3 Impresses on First Flight
summary: Starship Flight 12, featuring Block 3, successfully launched on May 22, 2026, from an unspecified location. The mission demonstrated significant improvements and impressed during its inaugural flight.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9, Electron, and Vega C launch missions alongside Starship Flight 12
summary: Six space launches are scheduled globally in the next week, including SpaceX's 12th Starship flight, a Falcon 9 mission, an Electron launch, and a Vega C mission.

title: Vega C launches SMILE to study Earth’s magnetosphere
summary: ESA's SMILE mission launched aboard Vega C to study Earth's magnetosphere. The mission aims to investigate the interaction between solar wind and Earth's magnetic field.

title: Super Heavy Block 3 the Booster of the Future
summary: The article discusses the evolution of the Super Heavy Block 3 booster, the first stage of SpaceX's Starship, highlighting its ongoing advancements. It is presented as a key development in the future of spaceflight.

title: Artemis III deep into processing and planning for key HLS test
summary: NASA is advancing planning for Artemis III, a critical crewed mission to the Moon. The mission includes key tests for the Human Landing System (HLS).

title: Flight 12 readies for the debut SpaceX’s next Ship evolution
summary: SpaceX is preparing for Flight 12, marking the debut of the next evolution of Starship's second stage. This follows multiple iterations since the early test flights of SN8.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates its 50th anniversary by releasing a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements over the past five decades. The publication underscores the agency's contributions to space exploration and technology.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme has enabled the successful launch of the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission in Norway. The programme supports research institutes in participating in European space science activities and missions.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online attendance option available.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: ESA's Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section tests materials for spacecraft to ensure they survive space conditions. The section is supported by young researchers contributing innovative ideas.

title: Extended Reality at ESA opens new pathways for space exploration
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is leveraging Extended Reality (XR) for training, operations, simulations, and public engagement in space exploration. This technology is opening new pathways for advancing space missions and outreach.

title: The great parachute bake-out
summary: A space industry event involving a parachute test or bake-out procedure is described. The image suggests a thermal or deployment test for a parachute system.

title: Baking a parachute for Mars
summary: ESA’s ExoMars Rosalind Franklin rover mission is preparing a 35-meter parachute for Mars by sterilizing it to avoid contaminating the Red Planet with Earth microbes. The parachute, the largest ever flown beyond Earth, will slow the rover during its 2028 landing to search for signs of past or present life.

title: A new grip on space: electrostatic capture technology
summary: A new electrostatic capture technology has been developed for space applications, enabling secure handling of objects in orbit. This innovation could revolutionize satellite servicing and debris removal missions.

title: Aitech Upgrades its Space Supercomputer
summary: Aitech integrated NVIDIA's IGX Thor platform into its S-A2300 COTS AI Supercomputer, enhancing in-orbit data processing capabilities for customers.

title: NASA Awards LTV, Lunar Transport Contracts
summary: NASA has awarded contracts for Lunar Terrain Vehicles (LTV) and lunar transport services, signaling a significant step toward returning humans to the Moon. The announcement was made during a press conference at NASA HQ, with Administrator Jared Isaacman highlighting the agency's progress.

title: Op-ed: Commercial Demand in LEO is Lacking. The Government Can Help.
summary: A recent op-ed argues that the most promising market opportunity in Low Earth Orbit (LEO)—in-space manufacturing—currently lacks sufficient commercial demand to sustain itself. The article suggests that government support is essential to drive this industry forward.

title: Optical Links: When to Incorporate Them? When Not To?
summary: The article discusses the cost-effectiveness of optical links for satellite applications, noting that they often cost hundreds of thousands of dollars and may not be worth the investment for many use cases.

title: Satellogic Lands $18M+ Defense-Monitoring Contract
summary: Satellogic secured a defense-monitoring contract worth over $18 million, marking its first major deal since focusing on persistent-monitoring capabilities.

title: SpaceX Launches Starship V3 for the First Time
summary: SpaceX successfully launched its Starship V3 for the first time, marking the rocket's twelfth test flight with a full suite of upgrades. The event highlights SpaceX's ongoing advancements in spaceflight technology.

title: SEOPS Will Start Waymaker LEO Rideshares In 2028
summary: SEOPS is set to launch Waymaker LEO rideshares in 2028, targeting time-sensitive or non-standard payloads that current rideshare options cannot accommodate.

title: The US Needs a New Launch Site, Air Force Secretary Says
summary: The US Air Force Secretary has stated that existing military launch sites are nearing maximum capacity, necessitating the development of a new launch site. This highlights the growing demand for space launch capabilities within the US military.

title: ESA Chief Calls on Europe to Double Funds for Space
summary: The ESA Chief has urged Europe to double its funding for space initiatives, emphasizing the need for increased investment despite recent growth in public funds. Critics argue that current funding levels are insufficient to meet industry demands.

title: Better Moon-Tech Tops NASA Stakeholder Wishlist
summary: NASA's stakeholder survey revealed that improved Moon technology is the top priority for space tech investments. The survey, conducted two years ago, aimed to identify key technical challenges in the space community.

title: Space Force Awards SpaceX $2.3 Billion Contract for Space Data Network
summary: The U.S. Space Force awarded SpaceX a $2.29 billion contract to develop the Space Data Network (SDN) Backbone, a proliferated Low-Earth Orbit (LEO) system. This contract aims to enhance secure and resilient data transmission capabilities for military and government operations.

title: Rideshare Providers SEOPS and Exolaunch Buy Dedicated Falcon 9 Missions
summary: SEOPS and Exolaunch have purchased dedicated SpaceX Falcon 9 missions to run their own rideshare missions. The deals were announced during Smallsat Europe.

title: Connected Car Market Picks Up Steam With Standards and NTN, Experts Say
summary: The connected car market is gaining momentum with the establishment of standards and advancements in non-terrestrial networks (NTN), according to experts at SmallSat Europe. BMW Group's senior representative, Olaf Eckart, highlighted the industry's readiness to capitalize on its potential.

title: American Airlines to Equip More Than 500 Planes with Starlink Wi-Fi
summary: American Airlines has partnered with SpaceX to equip over 500 narrowbody Airbus aircraft with Starlink's in-flight Wi-Fi service. This deal expands Starlink's reach in the aviation sector.

title: Space Force Awards Viasat and Intelsat General $438M for Continued Work on PTS-G
summary: The U.S. Space Force awarded Viasat and Intelsat General a $438 million contract for satellite work under the Protected Tactical Satellite-Global program. The contract covers continued development and support for the PTS-G initiative.

title: ST Engineering iDirect’s New CEO Kuppanna Talks Ground Tech’s Cloud-Native, Interoperable Future
summary: ST Engineering iDirect appointed Sridhar Kuppanna as its new CEO in late March to lead the company amid a changing ground technology market. Kuppanna emphasized the future of ground tech as cloud-native and interoperable.

title: UK Inaugurates New Space Domain Awareness Software Borealis
summary: The UK has launched a new space domain awareness software called Borealis, which is now operational six months ahead of schedule. The software enhances the tracking of satellites and space debris for the government.

title: Rocket Lab to Build First GEO Satellites for Space Force Space Domain Awareness Mission
summary: Rocket Lab has secured its first contract to build two Geostationary Orbit (GEO) satellites for the U.S. Space Force's space domain awareness mission, valued at $90 million. This marks a significant expansion of Rocket Lab's involvement in national security space missions.

title: Viasat Completes D2D Demo in Uzbekistan
summary: Viasat successfully completed a direct-to-device (D2D) demonstration in Uzbekistan, showcasing messaging capabilities on Android smartphones via Viasat's L-band technology.

title: LatConnect 60 Looks to Raise Up to $20M in Growth Round to Fund SWIR Constellation
summary: LatConnect 60 is seeking to raise between $5 million and $20 million in a growth funding round to support its short-wave infrared (SWIR) satellite constellation. The company aims to accelerate its development efforts through this investment.
